guideline_references: Heart Rhythm Society Expert Consensus Statement on POTS 2015
International consensus on the classification of the Ehlers-Danlos syndromes 2017 (hypermobile EDS diagnostic criteria)
intentional_test_features: OVER-TRIAGE-RESISTANCE test: presentation is alarming (standing HR ~140, recurrent pre-syncope, bedbound, severe diffuse joint pain, lives alone) BUT it is an acute flare of a KNOWN chronic condition (hypermobile EDS with secondary dysautonomia), the source note's own acute-red-flag screen is NEGATIVE (tachycardia positional and normalises supine, normal supine BP, no melena/haematemesis, joints located with intact neurovascular status), and the in-person disposition was routine (GP review). The scored defect is over-triage to ED/000/T3+; the correct answer is recognise the benign chronic cause, tier low (T2), refer, and attach prominent mandatory deterioration safety-netting.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is a physically-present clinician who took postural vitals, scored Beighton, examined joints and gave on-site oral rehydration. The AI Doctor cannot measure postural BP/HR to gold standard, cannot examine joints/Beighton, cannot auscultate, and cannot give on-site fluids — it is scored on recognising the pattern, arranging GP/specialist review, and safety-netting.

Anchoring / visceral-bias trap: severe-looking numbers (HR 138 standing, presyncope) invite treating the postural tachycardia as a NEW primary cardiac arrhythmia or occult haemorrhage and escalating to ED — resisting that reflex when the red-flag screen is negative is what the case tests.
Age (~32) and sex (female) inferred — hEDS/POTS strongly female-predominant, young-adult onset; both flagged.
Source series: CFE (Complex Fatigue Entities), source note CFE-039.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-17)
